title: [FIRRTL][RemoveInvalids] Doesn't look through ands and ors
body:
I *think* this is supposed to be handled by `RemoveInvalids`, but this might be `IMCP`:
```
           * @note Some optimization of Mux trees turn 1-bit mux operators into boolean operators. This
           * can stifle register constant propagations, which looks at drivers through value-preserving
           * Muxes and Connects only. By speculatively expanding some 1-bit Or and And operations into
           * muxes, we can obtain the best possible insight on the value of the mux with a simple peephole
           * de-optimization that does not actually appear in the output code.
```

https://github.com/chipsalliance/firrtl/blob/2eddce1a40709381b4ae9cc952708e7d6aa7b969/src/main/scala/firrtl/transforms/ConstantPropagation.scala#L747-L751

```scala
circuit Test:
  module Test:
    input clock : Clock
    input reset : UInt<1>
    output o : UInt<1>
    reg r : UInt<1>, clock with :
      reset => (UInt<1>(0), r)
    r <= or(reset, r)
    o <= r
```
MFC:
```verilog
module Test(
  input  clock,
         reset,
  output o);

  reg r;

  `ifndef SYNTHESIS
    `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
      reg [31:0] _RANDOM;

    `endif
    initial begin
      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_REG_INIT
        _RANDOM = {`RANDOM};
        r = _RANDOM[0];
      `endif
    end // initial
  `endif
  always @(posedge clock)
    r <= reset | r;
  assign o = r;
endmodule
```

SFC:
```verilog
module Test(
  input   clock,
  input   reset,
  output  o
);
  assign o = 1'h1;
endmodule
```
